data.title: Surge Battery Metals to raise $15M in non-brokered LIFE offering
data.description:
• Surge Battery Metals (NILI:CA) (TSXV: NILI) (OTCQX: NILIF) on Wednesday said that it will raise about $15 million through a non-brokered private placement under Canada’s listed issuer financing exemption.
  • The company will issue 16.67 million units at $0.90 each, with each unit comprising one common share and half of a share purchase warrant. Each full warrant will be exercisable at $1.35 per share, starting 60 days after issuance and expiring three years from issuance.
  • Proceeds will be used for costs related to the preliminary and definitive feasibility studies for its Nevada North Lithium Project and for general working capital.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about February 3, 2026, subject to TSX Venture Exchange approval.
  • NILIF closed -1.91% at $0.6808.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: AirJoule Technologies announces pricing of $20M public offering of Class A common stock
data.description:
• AirJoule Technologies (AIRJ) on Wednesday announced the pricing of an underwritten public offering of 6.15M shares of its Class A common stock, par value $0.0001 per share, at a public offering price of $3.25 per share.
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds it receives from the offering to fund growth capital, working capital, and for general corporate purposes.
  • In addition, the company has granted the underwriter a 45-day option to purchase up to an additional 923,077 shares of Class A common stock at the public offering price, less the underwriting discounts and commissions.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about January 15, 2026.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: United Community Banks Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 in-line, revenue of $278.4M beats by $4.8M
data.description:
• United Community Banks press release (UCB): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.71 in-line.
  • Revenue of $278.4M (+16.3% Y/Y) beats by $4.8M.
  • United’s return on assets was 1.21%, or 1.22% on an operating basis, up from 1.06% and 1.08%, respectively for the fourth quarter of 2024. Return on common equity was 9.5% and return on tangible common equity on an operating basis was 13.3%. On a pre-tax, pre-provision basis, operating return on assets was 1.78% for the quarter. At year-end, tangible common equity to tangible assets was 9.92%, up 21 basis points from the third quarter.
  • Provision for credit losses was $13.7 million, up $2.3 million from a year ago and up $5.8 million from the third quarter; allowance for credit losses coverage down slightly to 1.16% of total loans; net charge-offs were $16.4 million, or 0.34% of average loans, annualized
  •

data.description:

data.title: Wingstop reinstates COO role
data.description:
• Wingstop (WING) reinstated the role of chief operating officer with the appointment of Rajneesh Kapoor, previously president of international.
  • Kapoor will oversee the company’s domestic and international franchise development and operations, as well as the company-owned and operated restaurants.

data.title: UnitedHealth to cut Medicare Advantage payment timelines by 50% in rural hospitals
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group’s (UNH) insurance arm, UnitedHealthcare, on Wednesday announced a pilot program targeting independent rural hospitals to accelerate their Medicare Advantage payment timelines by 50%.

The company, the number one MA insurer in the U.S., plans to run the program in Oklahoma, Idaho, Minnesota, and Missouri over a six-month period and intends to expand it to additional hospitals and markets depending on insights gathered.

The program titled Rural Payment Acceleration Pilot aims to reduce the MA payment period to less than 15 days from less than 30 days, providing immediate cash flow relief for these rural hospitals facing financial challenges, UnitedHealthcare said.

“By speeding up payments to these critical facilities, we’re helping providers focus on what matters most: patient care,” said Bobby Hunter, CEO of UnitedHealthcare Government Programs. “Together, we will learn quickly and scale what works.”

data.title: Spot silver surges past $90 for first time, gold nears all-time peak on Fed bets
data.description:
Spot silver (XAGUSD:CUR) surged past the key $90-an-ounce mark for the first time, driven by softer U.S. inflation data that reinforced expectations for Federal Reserve rate cuts, alongside geopolitical tensions, strong industrial and investment demand, and tightening supplies.

Silver prices jumped more than 4.8% to $91.13 an ounce at press time. Gold spot price (XAUUSD:CUR) also edged above 1% higher to $4,634.9 an ounce, close to an all-time peak.

Underlying U.S. inflation in December came in lower than expected, though economists cautioned the data may have been artificially subdued due to the record-long government shutdown late last year.

Precious metals have built on last year’s gains as the possibility of a criminal indictment against Jerome Powell reignited concerns over central bank independence. Global policymakers have voiced support for Powell, while Jamie Dimon of JPMorgan Chase & Co. warned that political interference could backfire.

Safe-haven demand has also been supported by U.S. President Donald Trump’s capture of Venezuela’s leader, renewed threats to seize Greenland, and violent protests in Iran that could destabilize the Islamic regime.

Against this backdrop, analysts at Citigroup raised their three-month forecasts for gold and silver to $5,000 an ounce and $100 an ounce, respectively.

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS),(GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

data.title: Gold Royalty buys additional Borborema royalty from Dundee in $45M deal
data.description:
Gold Royalty (GROY) said pre-market Wednesday it agreed to acquire a net smelter royalty on the Borborema gold mine and property in Brazil from Dundee Corp. (DDEJF) for $45M, comprised of $30M in cash and more than 3.57M newly issued common shares.

The royalty applies to the Borborema project operated by Aura Minerals (AUGO), and the royalty rate is 1.5% of net smelter returns on the first 1.5M oz of payable gold production and 1.0% until 2M oz of payable gold is produced.

Gold Royalty (GROY) already holds a 2.0% net smelter return royalty on the Borborema mine, which steps down to 0.5% after 725K oz of payable gold production; the existing royalty is subject to operator buyback rights after 2.25M oz are produced, or by 2050.

Aura Minerals (AUGO) achieved commercial production at the mine in September, operating at 80% of design capacity and processing 4,500 metric tons/day.
